title: Third Impact Scenarios
wikitext:
[]: Third Impact Scenarios             Quote Post by Tricalibur » Fri Jul 29, 2011 1:43 pm Hi everybody. This is my first time posting here. I’ve lurked for a couple months now and love reading the intellectual conversations about this anime series. I’m currently working on an Eva inspired metal album and as part of my research have been watching the series / movies and trying to understand what I can of the story. This post will be geared mostly towards NGE and more specifically episodes 14 on through EOE. I’m really confused about the multiple different scenarios that are presented for Third Impact and the Human Instrumentality Project. From what I can gather there are four main scenarios: the Angel’s scenario, Seeles’s scenario, Ikari’s scenario, and what actually happens in EOE. Here they are in detail: 1) The Angel’s Scenario – Angel comes into contact with Adam / Lilith and causes extinction of humanity. Not sure what happens after this. 2) Seele’s Scenario – Use the Eva series to start the Human Instrumentality project. Unit 1 is important to this? 3) Ikari’s Scenario – I am very confused about this one. From what I can gather he wants to use Rei as some sort of vector to transplant the embryo of Adam into Lilith and then join with Unit 01 to be with Yui. I guess in his scenario it was intended for the two of them to float into space together, rather than just Yui as actually happened in EOE. In this scenario Human Instrumentality would still have taken place. It was necessary for Unit 1 to awaken, or become “aware,” as Ritsuko called it. By this I assume she meant become aware that her child was the pilot. But this raises another question: Where does Shinji fit into Ikari’s plan? Why was it necessary for Yui to recognize Shinji as the pilot? 4) Shinji / Yui’s Scenario – This is what actually happened in EOE. The Eva series is used to initiate Third Impact with the awakened Unit 01 as it’s “catalyst.” Meanwhile, Rei/Yui rejects Ikari’s plan and fuses herself and the Adam embryo with Lilith. This creates a whole Lilith comprised of the physical being that was held in Terminal Dogma and the soul portion contained within Rei. Shinji is basically given the power of God by Rei/Lilith and is entrusted with the decision of whether humanity should continue existing. Yui then floats into space forever trapped inside Unit 01. Is it right to consider Rei as a separate entity from Yui? A lot of the decisions made by Rei in EOE make more sense when thought about from the context of her being a Yui clone. Each scenario has its own mysteries and inconsistencies that aren’t explained very well in the series, but I’d like to hear from you guys what your opinions of them are. Fell free to correct me or add to what I’ve compiled here, I’m just interested in learning as much as I can about the Third Impact in the context of NGE.

[]: Re: Third Impact Scenarios             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jul 29, 2011 2:08 pm [url]http://wiki.evageeks.org/Third%20Impact[/url] is of help here. Tricalibur wrote: The Angel’s Scenario – Angel comes into contact with Adam / Lilith and causes extinction of humanity. Not sure what happens after this.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 29, 2011 2:10 pm You should check out this thread, too: My Big Honkin' "Mechanics of the Impacts" Theories

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Jul 29, 2011 3:48 pm ^ I remember reading through that series of posts. Someone should make that a sticky. @OP: Right on pretty much all counts. Shinji was thrown into this web of subterfuge purely because Gendo had no other options. And Rei is different from Yui, yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Tricalibur » Fri Jul 29, 2011 7:53 pm Azathoth, thanks for the reply, very insightful. To answer your question, it's progressive power metal sort of like Maiden meets Sabbath meets Mastodon.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jul 29, 2011 9:16 pm Tricalibur wrote: To answer your question, it's progressive power metal sort of like Maiden meets Sabbath meets Mastodon.

[]:             Quote Post by Tricalibur » Sat Jul 30, 2011 9:40 am Check the link in my sig. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Petrucio » Sat Jul 30, 2011 11:50 pm Reflect on this song from Rebuild before you read the definitive explanation on this subject: [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=buobW1zVQuQ[/url] "In the end the final decision we all must take, make or break, will decide what will be and our destiny. If we lose the fight armageddon will finally tell, burn in hell, there will be no after, be no other day." SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Third Impact Scenarios             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jul 31, 2011 6:30 am Azathoth wrote: It wasn't. Gendou didn't want to involve Shinji at all - hence the poor guy gets dumped in the shit with no prior instruction. The fact of the matter is that he doesn't have another pilot for Unit 01, he needs to kill the Angels, and Yui likes Shinji. He has no choice but to put Shinji in there.

[]: Re: Third Impact Scenarios             Quote Post by Petrucio » Sun Jul 31, 2011 8:19 am Jornophelanthas, Thank you, that was brilliant. I just don't agree with the conclusions. You got as far as understanding what the characters say with their words. But you are denying the obvious fact that Shinji and Asuka are not in Kansas anymore in the end. There was a lot of self deception on the part of Shinji during instrumentality. But the scene where he tries to kill Asuka speaks louder to me than his rationalizations for wanting to be an individual and believing he will find fulfillment this way. If you want hope for the hero, than I offer you the alternative hypothesis that EoE was still instrumentality for him. That wasn't really the end of instrumentality, Rei will try to convince him with the ultimate thought love session, and then maybe he'll break.

[]: Re: Third Impact Scenarios             Quote Post by Tricalibur » Sun Jul 31, 2011 9:19 am Jornophelanthas wrote: Gendo is motivated by his own vision of eternal bliss for himself

[]: Re: Third Impact Scenarios             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jul 31, 2011 9:48 am Tricalibur wrote: I don't agree that Gendo would have wanted Shinji inside Unit 01 with him and Yui. At no point in the series or EoE does he make it even slightly apparent that he gives a crap about Shinji. He's just a means to an end for Gendo. Even in flashback sequences prior to Yui's death we don't see Gendo showing a real fatherly love for Shinji. However, I do agree that Gendo's real intention in the context of Instrumentality / Third Impact was to create a sort of personal heaven for himself. Rather than becoming a God, I think that Gendo just wanted to create a living paradise for himself and his wife where no one else could interfere. It would be interesting if we knew more about Gendo's past from the series so we could really get an idea of what sort of person he was. I find it hard to believe that he was always a cold emotionless bastard as he is in almost every scene of the series. Edit: He does show concern for Rei II during the Unit 0 activation tests, but by the end of the series we find that he is unconcerned with her life as well (i.e. Why didn't he freak out when she tried to kill herself in Ep. 23 as well?) For me it just goes to show that he is a selfish person who cares only for his personal happiness and lends credibility to the "he only wanted to be with Yui in Unit 01" theory.

[]: Re: Third Impact Scenarios             Quote Post by Petrucio » Sun Jul 31, 2011 10:19 am Jornophelanthas wrote: In short, Gendo honestly believes that he loves his son, and honestly believes that everything he does will make Shinji just as happy as it will make himself happy.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jul 31, 2011 10:29 am Petrucio wrote: Jornophelanthas, Thank you, that was brilliant. I just don't agree with the conclusions. You got as far as understanding what the characters say with their words. But you are denying the obvious fact that Shinji and Asuka are not in Kansas anymore in the end. There was a lot of self deception on the part of Shinji during instrumentality. But the scene where he tries to kill Asuka speaks louder to me than his rationalizations for wanting to be an individual and believing he will find fulfillment this way.

[]:             Quote Post by Petrucio » Sun Jul 31, 2011 10:49 am Very nice, but, Jornophelanthas wrote: Unit-01 drifts away into space, carrying only Yui's soul, to remain alone for all eternity as a testament to the existence of humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jul 31, 2011 11:04 am Petrucio wrote: Very nice, but, What's Fuyutsuki doing there talking with her under a tree, in an environment full of life?

[]:             Quote Post by Petrucio » Sun Jul 31, 2011 11:15 am Jornophelanthas wrote: The scene with the tree and the blue sky are a memory of pre-Third Impact earth, not an alternative reality to the red earth Shinji enters shortly afterwards.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Sun Jul 31, 2011 12:43 pm The narrator.

[]: Re: Third Impact Scenarios             Quote Post by Tricalibur » Sun Jul 31, 2011 1:34 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: This is why I disagree with Tricalibur, quoted above.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Jul 31, 2011 2:02 pm Petrucio wrote: Yes, but who is remembering that memory? Yui inside the Eva-01, or Yui inside Rei?
